title: Write a Parliamentary Submission
description: Write a formal submission to a current parliamentary inquiry — identify the inquiry, research the terms of reference, present evidence-based arguments, and submit through the official channel.
whyItMatters: Parliamentary submissions are how citizens and organisations directly influence legislation. Committee members and staff read every submission. A well-crafted submission can change the course of a bill or shape recommendations in a committee report.

steps.title: Find an active inquiry
steps.description: Search the APH website for current Senate or House committee inquiries. Choose one that interests you and is still accepting submissions.
steps.tips: Senate inquiries often have more impact than House inquiries because the Senate is where legislation is scrutinised.

steps.title: Study the terms of reference
steps.description: Read the terms of reference carefully. Your submission must address the specific questions the committee has asked.
steps.tips: Answer the terms of reference directly. Don't go off on tangents no matter how passionately you feel about them.

steps.title: Research and draft
steps.description: Write a 3-5 page submission: introduce yourself and your expertise, present evidence addressing each relevant term of reference, and make specific recommendations.
steps.tips: Number your recommendations. Committees find it easier to adopt numbered, specific recommendations.

steps.title: Cite sources
steps.description: Support every claim with evidence — data, research, case studies, expert testimony.
steps.tips: Link to publicly available sources where possible.

steps.title: Review and submit
steps.description: Have someone review your submission for clarity and tone. Submit through the official APH portal before the deadline.
steps.tips: Keep the tone respectful and constructive. You are helping the committee do its job, not lecturing them.

deliverables: A cover letter to the committee secretariat
Confirmation of submission via the APH portal

rubric.description: Does the submission directly address the committee's specific questions?
Are claims supported by credible, verifiable data and research?
Are the recommendations specific, numbered, and actionable?
Is the submission respectful, constructive, and properly formatted?
Does the submission provide genuine value to the committee's deliberations?
